1.texto_original: Sucedeu depois disto que David consultou ao Senhor, dizendo: Subirei a alguma das cidades de Judá? Respondeu-lhe o Senhor: Sobe. Ainda perguntou David: Para onde subirei? Respondeu o Senhor: Para Hebron.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Samuel 2:1

"Sucedeu depois disto que David consultou ao Senhor, dizendo: Subirei a alguma das cidades de Judá? Respondeu-lhe o Senhor: Sobe. Ainda perguntou David: Para onde subirei? Respondeu o Senhor: Para Hebron."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Samuel 2:1

Philological Analysis

"Sucedeu depois disto que David consultou ao Senhor, dizendo: Subirei a alguma das cidades de Judá? Respondeu-lhe o Senhor: Sobe. Ainda perguntou David: Para onde subirei? Respondeu o Senhor: Para Hebron."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Sucedeu depois disto que David consultou ao Senhor, dizendo: Subirei a alguma das cidades de Judá? Respondeu-lhe o Senhor: Sobe. Ainda perguntou David: Para onde subirei? Respondeu o Senhor: Para Hebron."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Exegetical Analysis of 2 Samuel 2:28

Philological Analysis

"Então Joab tocou a buzina, e todo o povo parou; e não perseguiram mais a Israel, e tampouco pelejaram mais."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
28.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Então Joab tocou a buzina, e todo o povo parou; e não perseguiram mais a Israel, e tampouco pelejaram mais."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

29.texto_original: E caminharam Abner e os seus homens toda aquela noite pela Arabá; e, passando o Jordão, caminharam por todo o Bitron, e vieram a Maanaim.
29.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Samuel 2:29

"E caminharam Abner e os seus homens toda aquela noite pela Arabá; e, passando o Jordão, caminharam por todo o Bitron, e vieram a Maanaim."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

30.texto_original: Voltou, pois, Joab de seguir a Abner; e quando ajuntou todo o povo, faltavam dos servos de David dezanove homens, e Asael.

31.texto_original: Mas os servos de David tinham ferido dentre os de Benjamim, e dentre os homens de Abner, a trezentos e sessenta homens, de tal maneira que morreram.

32.texto_original: E levantaram a Asael, e o sepultaram no sepulcro de seu pai, que estava em Belém. E Joab e seus homens caminharam toda aquela noite, e amanheceu-lhes o dia em Hebron.
32.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Samuel 2:32

"E levantaram a Asael, e o sepultaram no sepulcro de seu pai, que estava em Belém. E Joab e seus homens caminharam toda aquela noite, e amanheceu-lhes o dia em Hebron."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.
